question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The atmosphere is heated more by incoming solar radiation than by terrestrial radiation. Statement-II: Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere are good absorbers of long wave radiation. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
(c) Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
(d) Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
answer_explanation: The atmosphere is primarily heated from below by terrestrial (long-wave) radiation, making Statement-I incorrect. [cite: 1] [cite_start] Greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide are largely transparent to incoming short-wave solar radiation but effectively absorb the outgoing long-wave terrestrial radiation. [cite: 2]
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 9: Solar Radiation, Heat Balance and Temperature.
subject: Geography
assumptions: Assuming standard UPSC interpretation of atmospheric heating dynamics.

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Thickness of the troposphere at the equator is much greater as compared to poles. Statement-II: At the equator, heat is transported to great heights by strong convectional currents. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
(c) Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
(d) Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
answer_explanation: The troposphere's thickness is roughly 18 km at the equator and 8 km at the poles, making Statement-I correct. [cite: 5] [cite_start] This difference exists because strong convectional currents transport heat to greater heights at the equator, which directly explains the phenomenon. [cite: 6]
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 8: Composition and Structure of Atmosphere.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Pyroclastic debris 2. Ash and dust 3. Nitrogen compounds 4. Sulphur compounds. How many of the above are products of volcanic eruptions?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) Only three
(d) All four
answer_explanation: Volcanic eruptions release a wide variety of materials. [cite_start]Solid materials include pyroclastic debris, ash, and dust. [cite: 8] [cite_start]The gaseous emissions heavily feature sulphur compounds (like sulphur dioxide) and nitrogen compounds. [cite: 8]
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 3: Interior of the Earth.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Which of the following is/are correct inference/inferences from isothermal maps in the month of January? 1. The isotherms deviate to the north over the ocean and to the south over the continent. 2. The presence of cold ocean currents, Gulf Stream and North Atlantic Drift make the North Atlantic Ocean colder and the isotherms bend towards the north.
options_combined:
(a) 1 only
(b) 2 only
(c) Both 1 and 2
(d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: In January, the Northern Hemisphere experiences winter, meaning oceans are warmer than landmasses; thus, isotherms deviate north over oceans and south over continents. [cite: 9] [cite_start] Statement 2 is incorrect because the Gulf Stream and North Atlantic Drift are warm ocean currents, not cold ones. [cite: 10]
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 9: Solar Radiation, Heat Balance and Temperature.
subject: Geography
assumptions: Assumes the first statement implicitly refers to the Northern Hemisphere, which is standard in UPSC context for this specific wording.

question_text_combined: Which of the following countries are well known as the two largest cocoa producers in the world?
options_combined:
(a) Algeria and Morocco
(b) Botswana and Namibia
(c) Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana
(d) Madagascar and Mozambique
answer_explanation: West Africa dominates global cocoa production. [cite_start]Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana are consistently ranked as the top two largest cocoa producers in the world, accounting for the majority of global supply. [cite: 12]
answer_sources: FAOSTAT (Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations) global production data.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: With reference to the Himalayan rivers 9. joining the Ganga downstream of Prayagraj from West to East, which one of the following sequences is correct?
options_combined:
(a) Ghaghara - Gomati - Gandak-Kosi
(b) Gomati Ghaghara - Gandak - Kosi
(c) Ghaghara-Gomati - Kosi - Gandak
(d) Gomati Ghaghara Kosi - Gandak
answer_explanation: The correct West to East sequence of these Ganga tributaries is Gomati, Ghaghara, Gandak, and Kosi[cite: 13].  Gomati joins near Varanasi, Ghaghara near Chhapra, Gandak near Hajipur/Patna, and Kosi near Katihar.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 India: Physical Environment, Chapter 3: Drainage System.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Rainfall is one of the reasons for weathering of rocks. Statement-II: Rain water contains carbon dioxide in solution. Statement-III: Rain water contains atmospheric oxygen. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct and both of them explain Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement-I
(c) Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement-I
(d) Neither Statement-II nor Statement-III is correct
answer_explanation: Rainfall causes chemical weathering of rocks[cite: 14]. [cite_start]This happens because rainwater contains dissolved carbon dioxide (forming weak carbonic acid leading to carbonation) and dissolved oxygen (leading to oxidation of minerals)[cite: 15]. [cite_start]Both statements II and III are correct and explain the process in Statement I[cite: 16].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 6: Geomorphic Processes.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following countries: 1. Finland 2. Germany 3. Norway 4. Russia. How many of the above countries have a border with the North Sea?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) Only three
(d) All four
answer_explanation: Only two of the listed countries border the North Sea: Germany and Norway.  Finland and Russia border the Baltic Sea and the Arctic Ocean, not the North Sea.
answer_sources: World Atlas / Standard Political Map of Europe.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following information: Waterfall Region River 1.Dhuandhar Malwa Narmada 2.Hundru Chota Nagpur Subarnarekha 3.Gersoppa Western Ghats Netravati. In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: Only one row is correctly matched: Hundru Falls is on the Subarnarekha River in the Chota Nagpur plateau. Dhuandhar is on the Narmada but located in the Mahakoshal region (Jabalpur), not Malwa. [cite_start]Gersoppa (Jog Falls) is in the Western Ghats but is formed by the Sharavati River, not the Netravati[cite: 18].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 India: Physical Environment, Chapter 3: Drainage System.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following information: Region Name of the mountain range Type of mountain 1. Central Asia Vosges Fold mountain 2.Europe Alps Block mountain 3.North America Appalachians Fold mountain 4.South America Andes Fold mountain. In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) Only three
(d)All four
answer_explanation: Only two pairs are correct: The Appalachians and the Andes are both fold mountains[cite: 21]. [cite_start]The Vosges are block mountains located in Europe, and the Alps are fold mountains, making the first two pairs incorrect[cite: 20].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter: Landforms and their Evolution.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following airports: 1. Donyi Polo Airport 2. Kushinagar International Airport 3. Vijayawada International Airport. In the recent past, which of the above have been constructed as Greenfield projects?
options_combined:
(a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: The options are ambiguous because the standard answer choices for 'how many' style questions are missing, and only '1 only' is factually fully defensible, which is missing from the options. [cite_start]Donyi Polo is a Greenfield project[cite: 23]. Kushinagar and Vijayawada are upgrades of existing airstrips/airports (Brownfield). If forced, a candidate might choose (a) assuming Kushinagar's massive international upgrade classified it differently in some ministry reports, but structurally it is brownfield.
answer_sources: Ministry of Civil Aviation, Government of India: List of Greenfield Airports.
subject: Economy
assumptions: Marked ambiguous due to lack of a '1 only' option while Kushinagar is historically an existing airstrip.

question_text_combined: With reference to "water vapour", which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. It is a gas, the amount of which decreases with altitude. 2. Its percentage is maximum at the poles.
options_combined:
(a) 1 only
(b) 2 only
(c) Both 1 and 2
(d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: Water vapour decreases rapidly with altitude, making statement 1 correct[cite: 24]. [cite_start]Its percentage is highest near the equator and decreases towards the poles due to temperature differences, making statement 2 incorrect[cite: 24].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 8: Composition and Structure of Atmosphere.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following description: 1. Annual and daily range of temperatures is low. 2. Precipitation occurs throughout the year. 3. Precipitation varies between 50 cm 250 cm. What is this type of climate?
options_combined:
(a) Equatorial climate
(b) China type climate
(c) Humid subtropical climate
(d) Marine West coast climate
answer_explanation: The Marine West Coast climate features low temperature ranges due to oceanic influence and consistent precipitation throughout the year ranging from 50 to 250 cm[cite: 26]. [cite_start]Equatorial climates also have rain all year but typically experience much higher precipitation volumes[cite: 27].
answer_sources: G.C. Leong, Certificate Physical and Human Geography, Chapter 22: The Warm Temperate Western Margin Climate.
subject: Geography
assumptions: Assumed 50-250cm distinguishes it from the heavier rainfall parameters of a true Equatorial climate.

question_text_combined: With reference to "Coriolis force", which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. It increases with increase in wind velocity. 2. It is maximum at the poles and is absent at the equator.
options_combined:
(a) 1 only
(b) 2 only
(c) Both 1 and 2
(d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: The Coriolis force is directly proportional to wind velocity, making statement 1 correct[cite: 28]. [cite_start]It is governed by the sine of the latitude, meaning it is maximum at the poles (sin 90° = 1) and zero at the equator (sin 0° = 0), making statement 2 correct[cite: 28].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Fundamentals of Physical Geography, Chapter 10: Atmospheric Circulation and Weather Systems.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: On June 21 every year, which of the following latitude(s) experience(s) a sunlight of more than 12 hours? 1. Equator 2. Tropic of Cancer 3. Tropic of Capricorn 4. Arctic Circle
options_combined:
(a) 1 only
(b) 2 only
(c) 3 and 4
(d) 2 and 4
answer_explanation: On June 21 (Summer Solstice in the Northern Hemisphere), the sun is directly overhead the Tropic of Cancer, leading to daylight hours longer than 12 hours there[cite: 30]. [cite_start] The Arctic Circle experiences 24 hours of daylight[cite: 30]. The Equator has exactly 12 hours, and the Tropic of Capricorn has less than 12.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 6 The Earth Our Habitat, Chapter 3: Motions of the Earth.
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: One of the following regions has the world's largest tropical peatland, which holds about three years worth of global carbon emissions from fossil fuels; and the possible destruction of which can exert detrimental effect on the global climate. Which one of the following denotes that region?
options_combined:
(a) Amazon Basin
(b) Congo Basin
(c) Kikori Basin
(d) Rio de la Plata Basin
answer_explanation: The Congo Basin houses the Cuvette Centrale, recognized as the world's largest tropical peatland[cite: 31]. [cite_start]Its destruction would release massive amounts of sequestered carbon into the atmosphere, accelerating climate change[cite: 32].
answer_sources: UNEP (United Nations Environment Programme) publications on the Cuvette Centrale peatlands.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) that are used in making many consumer products, consider the following statements: 1. PFAS are found to be widespread in drinking water, food and food packaging materials. 2. PFAS are not easily degraded in the environment. 3. Persistent exposure to PFAS can lead to bioaccumulation in animal bodies. Which of the statements given above are correct?
options_combined:
(a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: PFAS are widespread synthetic chemicals often termed 'forever chemicals' because they resist environmental degradation[cite: 33]. [cite_start]They are commonly found in water and packaging, and they bioaccumulate in humans and animals over time[cite: 34].
answer_sources: US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) documentation on PFAS.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Carabid beetles 2. Centipedes 3. Flies 4. Termites 5. Wasps. Parasitoid species are found in how many of the above kind of organisms?
options_combined:
(a) Only two
(b) Only three
(c) Only four
(d) All five
answer_explanation: Parasitoid lifestyles are well documented in Wasps, Flies (e.g., Tachinid flies), and certain Beetles (e.g., Rove beetles or some Carabids). Centipedes are predators, and termites are social herbivores/detritivores; neither have parasitoid species.
answer_sources: Standard biological taxonomy texts on parasitoidism.
subject: Environment
assumptions: Assuming strict biological definitions of 'parasitoid' vs 'predator'.

question_text_combined: Consider the following plants: 1. Groundnut 2. Horse-gram 3. Soybean. How many of the above belong to the pea family?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: Groundnut, horse-gram, and soybean are all leguminous plants. They belong to the Fabaceae family, which is commonly known as the pea, legume, or bean family.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Biology, Chapter 5: Morphology of Flowering Plants (Family Fabaceae).
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: The Indian Flying Fox is placed under the "vermin" category in the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972. Statement-II: The Indian Flying Fox feeds on the blood of other animals. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
(c) Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
(d) Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
answer_explanation: The Indian Flying Fox is a fruit bat (frugivore) and does not feed on blood, making Statement-II unequivocally incorrect[cite: 37]. Under the original unamended WPA 1972, fruit bats were listed in Schedule V as vermin (though the 2022 amendment removed this schedule entirely). [cite_start]Assuming UPSC references the original act's historical framing, only Statement I is treated as correct[cite: 38].
answer_sources: Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 (Original schedules vs 2022 Amendment).
subject: Environment
assumptions: UPSC relied on the pre-2022 WPA 1972 classification for Statement I.

question_text_combined: The organisms "Cicada, Froghopper and Pond skater" are:
options_combined:
(a) Birds
(b) Fish
(c) Insects
(d) Reptiles
answer_explanation: Cicadas, froghoppers, and pond skaters are all classified biologically as insects (Class Insecta).
answer_sources: Standard biological taxonomy texts.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-1: Many chewing gums found in the market are considered a source of environmental pollution. Statement-II: Many chewing gums contain plastic as gum base. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are Statement-II explains correct and Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
(c) Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
(d) Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
answer_explanation: Modern chewing gums often utilize synthetic polymers (plastics like polyethylene and polyvinyl acetate) in their gum bases[cite: 39]. [cite_start]This makes them non-biodegradable, directly causing environmental pollution when improperly discarded[cite: 40]. Thus, Statement II directly explains Statement I.
answer_sources: UNEP reports on microplastics and synthetic polymers in consumer goods.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Country Animal found in its natural habitat 1. Brazil Indri 2. Indonesia Elk 3. Madagascar Bonobo. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: None of the pairs are correct. [cite_start]The Indri is a lemur endemic to Madagascar, not Brazil[cite: 41]. Elks are native to North America and Eurasia, not Indonesia. [cite_start]Bonobos are endemic to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, not Madagascar[cite: 41].
answer_sources: IUCN Red List of Threatened Species for habitat distribution.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements regarding World Toilet Organization: 1. It is one of the agencies of the United Nations. 2. World Toilet Summit, World Toilet Day and World Toilet College are the initiatives of this organization, to inspire action to tackle the global sanitation crisis. 3. The main focus of its function is to grant funds to the least developed countries and developing countries to achieve the end of open defecation. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined:
(a) 2 only
(b) 3 only
(c) 1 and 2
(d) 2 and 3
answer_explanation: The World Toilet Organization is a global non-profit organization, not a UN agency[cite: 43]. [cite_start]It operates through advocacy and initiatives like the World Toilet Summit and World Toilet College[cite: 44]. [cite_start]It is an advocacy group and does not function as a grant-funding agency for countries[cite: 45].
answer_sources: World Toilet Organization official website (About Us).
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Lions do not have a particular breeding season. 2. Unlike most other big cats, cheetahs do not roar. 3. Unlike male lions, male leopards do not proclaim their territory by scent marking. Which of the statements given above are correct?
options_combined:
(a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: Lions are polyestrous and breed throughout the year, making statement 1 correct[cite: 46]. [cite_start]Cheetahs lack the physical adaptation in the larynx to roar; instead, they purr and chirp, making statement 2 correct[cite: 47]. [cite_start]Male leopards actively scent mark their territories using urine and scrape marks, making statement 3 incorrect[cite: 48].
answer_sources: Wildlife Conservation Society (WCS) species profiles for Lions, Cheetahs, and Leopards.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Which one of the following is the correct description of "100 Million Farmers"?
options_combined:
(a) It is a platform for accelerating the transition towards food and water systems that are net-zero (carbon), nature-positive and that aims to increase farmer resilience.
(b) It is an international alliance and a network of individuals and farming organisations interested in supporting and strengthening the development of the organic animal husbandry.
(c) It is a digital platform fully integrated with service providers and built on blockchain that lets buyers, sellers and third parties trade fertilizers quickly and securely.
(d) It is a platform with the mission of encouraging the farmers to form Farmer Product Organisations or Agribusiness Consortiums, thus facilitating the access to global open markets to sell their products.
answer_explanation: The '100 Million Farmers' initiative is a World Economic Forum platform aimed at transitioning the global food system towards net-zero and nature-positive practices[cite: 49]. [cite_start]It specifically focuses on building farmer resilience and sustainability[cite: 49].
answer_sources: World Economic Forum (WEF) official press release on '100 Million Farmers'.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1 Battery storage 2. Biomass generators 3. Fuel cells 4. Rooftop solar photovoltaic units. How many of the above are considered "Distributed Energy Resources"?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) Only three
(d) All four
answer_explanation: Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) are small-scale energy generation or storage technologies located close to the load they serve. This includes battery storage, biomass generators, fuel cells, and rooftop solar units.
answer_sources: International Energy Agency (IEA) definitions of Distributed Energy Resources.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Which one of the following shows a unique relationship with an insect that has coevolved with it and that is the only insect that can pollinate this tree?
options_combined:
(a) Fig
(b) Mahua
(c) Sandalwood
(d) Silk cotton
answer_explanation: Fig trees exhibit highly specific obligate mutualism with fig wasps[cite: 55]. Each species of fig is typically pollinated by a specific, coevolved species of fig wasp, and neither organism can survive without the other.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 Biology, Chapter 13: Organisms and Populations (Mutualism).
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Butterflies 2. Fish 3. Frogs. How many of the above have poisonous species among them?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: All three groups have poisonous species. The Monarch butterfly is poisonous due to toxins from milkweed. Pufferfish contain deadly tetrodotoxin. Poison dart frogs secrete potent lipophilic alkaloid toxins through their skin.
answer_sources: Standard biological taxonomy texts.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Cashew 2. Papaya 3. Red sanders. How many of the above trees are actually native to India?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: Only Red Sanders is native to India (endemic to the Eastern Ghats). Cashew was introduced to India by the Portuguese from Brazil. Papaya is native to Mesoamerica and South America.
answer_sources: Botanical Survey of India (BSI) Flora of India.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs), consider the following statements: 1. RTGs are miniature fission reactors. 2. RTGs are used for powering the onboard systems of spacecrafts. 3. RTGs can use Plutonium-238, which is a by-product of weapons development. Which of the statements given above are correct?
options_combined:
(a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: RTGs are not fission reactors; they generate electricity from the heat released by the natural radioactive decay of isotopes like Plutonium-238[cite: 59]. [cite_start]They are extensively used to power deep-space spacecraft, making statements 2 and 3 correct while 1 is factually flawed[cite: 58].
answer_sources: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive (NSSDCA) - RTG Fact Sheet.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Statement-I: Giant stars live much longer than dwarf stars. Statement-II: Compared to dwarf stars, giant stars have a greater rate of nuclear reactions. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options_combined:
(a) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II explains Statement-I
(b) Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
(c) Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
(d) Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
answer_explanation: Giant stars have significantly more mass, which causes them to burn their nuclear fuel at a much higher rate than dwarf stars[cite: 61]. [cite_start]Because of this rapid fuel consumption, their lifespans are drastically shorter, making Statement-I incorrect[cite: 62].
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Physical Geography, Chapter 1: The Earth and the Universe (Stellar Evolution).
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Which one of the following is synthesised in human body that dilates blood vessels and increases blood flow?
options_combined:
(a) Nitric oxide
(b) Nitrous oxide
(c) Nitrogen dioxide
(d) Nitrogen pentoxide
answer_explanation: Nitric oxide (NO) is an endogenously synthesized molecule in the human body[cite: 64]. It acts as a potent vasodilator, relaxing the inner muscles of blood vessels and increasing blood flow.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Biology, Chapter 18: Body Fluids and Circulation (Cardiovascular regulation).
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following activities: 1. Identification of narcotics on passengers at airports or in aircraft 2. Monitoring of precipitation 3. Tracking the migration of animals In how many of the above activities can the radars be used?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: Radar technology has broad applications. [cite_start]Terahertz and millimeter-wave radars can penetrate clothing and identify concealed illicit substances like narcotics[cite: 65]. Weather radars monitor precipitation, and specialized aeroecological radars track bird and bat migration.
answer_sources: ScienceDirect - Terahertz radar applications in security.
NOAA - Weather Radar fundamentals.
subject: Science & Tech
assumptions: Assumes 'identification' encompasses active radar scanning technologies like millimeter-wave scanners utilized in modern aviation security.

question_text_combined: Consider the following aircraft: 1. Rafael 2. MiG-29 3. Tejas MK-1 How many of the above are considered fifth generation fighter aircraft?
options_combined:
(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
answer_explanation: None of the listed aircraft belong to the fifth generation[cite: 66]. The Dassault Rafale and MiG-29 are classified as 4th or 4.5 generation fighters. The HAL Tejas MK-1 is a 4th generation light combat aircraft.
